sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Trading Systems Reacting to (VSDA) Volatility

feed.title: Leidos to buy power design firm ENTRUST for about $2.4 billion
feed.summary: Defense contractor Leidos Holdings announced its plan to acquire power design firm ENTRUST Solutions Group from Kohlberg for approximately $2.4 billion. This acquisition aims to enhance Leidos' engineering services for utilities and is expected to close by the end of the second quarter of 2026. Leidos anticipates that the deal will immediately boost its revenue growth and adjusted core profit margin, with further benefits to adjusted earnings per share in 2027.

feed.title: Ambiq Micro : Welcomes Bernard Banks to Board of Directors
feed.summary: Ambiq Micro, a leader in ultra-low-power semiconductor solutions for edge AI, has announced the appointment of Dr. Bernard Banks to its Board of Directors, effective January 5, 2026. Dr. Banks brings extensive experience in leadership, academia, and military service, including roles at West Point, Northwestern University's Kellogg School of Management, and currently as Director of Rice University's Doerr Institute for New Leaders. His expertise in organizational leadership and talent development is expected to be a valuable asset as Ambiq Micro expands its edge AI solutions globally.

feed.title: Leidos to spend $2.4B on firm helping protect North America's power
feed.summary: Kohlberg has announced the sale of its portfolio company, ENTRUST Solutions Group, to Leidos for approximately $2.4 billion. ENTRUST is a leading consulting and engineering services platform focused on utility and infrastructure markets, providing essential solutions to protect North America's critical power infrastructure. This transaction marks a successful exit for Kohlberg, enabling ENTRUST to continue its growth and expand its capabilities under Leidos.

feed.title: Artificial Intelligence at Bristol Myers Squibb – Two Use Cases
feed.summary: Bristol Myers Squibb (BMS) is leveraging artificial intelligence in two key areas: accelerating clinical trial operations and optimizing workforce talent mobility. Through partnerships with companies like NVIDIA and Accenture, BMS has developed platforms like Workbench to enhance patient enrollment, data integration, and trial management. Additionally, their partnership with Eightfold AI has led to MyGrowth, an AI-powered talent marketplace focused on skills-based matching for internal career development, significantly reducing hiring times and increasing internal mobility.

feed.title: Earnings Scheduled For January 26, 2026
feed.summary: This article lists companies scheduled to report their quarterly earnings on January 26, 2026. It provides estimated earnings per share and revenue figures for numerous companies reporting both before and after the opening bell, including Ryanair Hldgs, Steel Dynamics, AGNC Investment, and Nucor. The report helps investors prepare for upcoming financial disclosures.

feed.title: This Emerging Markets ETF Has a Slight ESG Tilt
feed.summary: The iShares ESG Aware MSCI Emerging Markets Index ETF (XSEM) offers broad exposure to emerging markets with a mild ESG tilt, closely tracking its parent index due to tight constraints. Despite its low 0.25% annual fee, which provides a performance advantage over peers, the fund may still include investments in sectors or companies that some ESG-focused investors might prefer to avoid. Geopolitical risks are inherent, and while it has outperformed the category average since its inception, its long-term returns are expected to align closely with peers, supported by its low fees.

feed.title: Cboe Global Markets names new executives as COO plans retirement
feed.summary: Cboe Global Markets announced key executive leadership changes as COO Chris Isaacson plans to retire. Scott Johnston has been appointed Executive Vice President and Chief Operating Officer, while Heidi Fischer will take on the role of Executive Vice President and Global Head of Equities and Spot Markets. These appointments are part of Cboe's ongoing globalization strategy, with new regional office leadership also being implemented.

feed.title: Hospitals are embracing pulsed field ablation. Here’s why
feed.summary: Hospitals like Mayo Clinic and Cleveland Clinic are investing in pulsed field ablation systems despite their higher costs. This adoption is driven by the advantages of faster procedures, increasing patient demand, and the potential for new revenue streams. The article highlights that these institutions are recognizing the significant benefits offered by this advanced medical technology.

feed.title: Celebrating Excellence: Honoring P&G’s Top-Performing External Business Partners
feed.summary: P&G has announced the recipients of its 2025 External Business Partner Excellence Awards, recognizing 40 top-performing suppliers for their exceptional contributions. These awards highlight partners who demonstrated excellence in commercial, operational, innovation, relationship, and citizenship aspects over the past 12-24 months. P&G's Chief Purchasing Officer, Ana Elena Marziano, emphasized that these awards underscore the company's belief in partnership-driven progress and the critical role these suppliers play in delivering superior products globally.

feed.title: Revolution Medicines Stock Sinks After Merck Deal Talks Collapse
feed.summary: Revolution Medicines (RVMD) stock dropped significantly after reported acquisition talks with Merck & Co (MRK) collapsed due to disagreements over valuation. Merck had reportedly been considering a deal valuing Revolution Medicines between $28 billion and $32 billion. Despite the setback, the door is not entirely closed, as talks could resume or other bidders might emerge, especially with upcoming clinical trial data releases for Revolution Medicines' cancer drug candidates.

feed.title: Leidos to acquire power design firm ENTRUST, bolstering its energy infrastructure portfolio – Company Announcement
feed.summary: Leidos (NYSE: LDOS) announced its definitive agreement to acquire ENTRUST Solutions Group from Kohlberg for approximately $2.4 billion, significantly expanding its energy infrastructure engineering business. This acquisition will double the size of Leidos' current $600 million energy infrastructure engineering business, adding new capabilities and clients across the power delivery spectrum for both gas and electric utilities. The transaction is projected to be immediately accretive to Leidos' revenue growth and adjusted EBITDA margin, with an expected closing by the end of the second quarter of 2026.

feed.title: ICE First Look at Mortgage Performance: Increased Refinance Activity Drives Mortgage Prepayments Back Toward 3.5-Year High
feed.summary: ICE Mortgage Technology's December 2025 "First Look" report indicates that lower interest rates led to a surge in refinance activity and mortgage prepayments, nearing a 3.5-year high. While early-stage delinquencies improved, late-stage delinquencies and foreclosure activity, especially for FHA and VA loans, saw increases. The national delinquency rate fell, but foreclosure starts and inventory rose year-over-year.

feed.title: Ford partners with Carhartt on dealership uniforms, co-branded Super Duty
feed.summary: Ford and Carhartt have launched a partnership that includes outfitting dealership technicians in training with durable uniforms. The program began in late 2025 and also features a co-branded Super Duty vehicle. The initiative aims to provide robust workwear for those in the automotive service industry.

feed.title: Steel Dynamics Stock (STLD) Hitting New Highs Into Earnings – What To Expect
feed.summary: Steel Dynamics (STLD) stock has reached new highs ahead of its earnings report, with consensus EPS expectations at $1.86 on $4.76B revenue. This is modestly above management's guided midpoint of $1.67 EPS, suggesting that execution at or slightly above guidance could meet market expectations, while a shortfall might pressure the stock. The upcoming earnings call will be crucial in determining whether the fourth quarter's operational constraints are temporary or indicate more persistent pricing pressures for 2026.

feed.title: Autonomous Military Aircraft Market Report 2026 Featuring Strategic Analysis of The Boeing Company, Lockheed Martin Corp., GE Aviation, Northrop Grumman Corp., BAE Systems and More
feed.summary: The autonomous military aircraft market is experiencing significant growth, driven by advancements in UAV technology, AI integration, and increasing defense budgets. Projected to grow from $4.61 billion in 2025 to $6.54 billion by 2030, the market is characterized by innovations from key players like Boeing, Lockheed Martin, and Shield AI. Asia-Pacific was the largest region in 2025, with Western Europe anticipated to be the fastest-growing.
